name: Mingrelian
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Mingrelian is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Mingrelian (მარგალური ნინა) is a Kartvelian (South Caucasian) language of western Georgia, written in the Georgian Mkhedruli script — a true ALPHABET (one letter per phoneme, no inherent vowel, unicameral), not an abugida. It uses the Georgian letters plus, in some conventions, additional signs (e.g. ჷ for the schwa /ə/ and ჸ for the glottal stop) that standard Georgian lacks. INPUT CONTRACT: Mkhedruli Mingrelian text (Unicode, NFC); the historical Asomtavruli and Nuskhuri scripts are out of scope. Its structural ancestry node is Proto-Kartvelian (see ccs.json). PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode ming1252), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode ming1252.

ancestors.notes: Kartvelian — the classification node this language sits under.
